methodsAttempted.query: site:waseda.jp Waseda University generative AI ChatGPT policy students academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned HTTP 402, so discovery continued with official-domain web search and direct HTTP fetches. Results found Waseda's university-wide generative-AI statement, an English translation, school-level notices, and non-policy AI course or research pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:waseda.jp 早稲田大学 生成AI ChatGPT 学生 方針; site:waseda.jp 早稲田大学 生成系AI 授業 レポート
methodsAttempted.notes: Japanese official-domain discovery found the university-wide 2023 statement, Commerce and Graduate School of Commerce guidance, faculty pages linking to GenAI report cautions, and official AI-related news or research pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:wnpspt.waseda.jp/student/supportanywhere 生成AI レポート 論文 留意点; site:wnpspt.waseda.jp/student_en/supportanywhere generative AI chatbot policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Official Support Anywhere student pages surfaced report/plagiarism guidance that directs students to follow instructor guidance on generative AI, and a chatbot notice explaining limits of AI-generated support answers.

Direct HTTP fetches succeeded for HTML sources on waseda.jp and wnpspt.waseda.jp. A Graduate School of Law PDF was discovered on an official Waseda URL but direct fetch returned HTTP 403 and it was marked inaccessible rather than used as evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found Waseda University's university-wide generative-AI statement in Japanese with an English translation, Commerce and Graduate School of Commerce student guidance, and official Support Anywhere student guidance. Claims are limited to those source scopes. Official AI courses, research/book pages, and an inaccessible law-program PDF were rejected or marked inaccessible and do not support claims.

About the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence (ChatGPT, etc.)
生成AIの利用について（商学部・商学研究科）
レポート等における不正行為の取り扱いについて教えてください。

artifacts.claimText: Waseda University's university-wide April 2023 statement warns students that submitting a paper created by generative AI as-is is treated as misconduct similar to cheating, and that plagiarism, inappropriate citation, or fabrication in submitted work can expose the student to disciplinary action.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Submitting unexamined or as-is generative-AI-created work can trigger academic misconduct consequences.

citation.citationTitle: 生成AIなどの利用について
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Waseda University's university-wide April 2023 statement warns that careless generative-AI use may leak confidential information from the university or companies and may also lead to personal-information leakage when prompts include a person's name or similar details.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Generative AI prompts can create confidential-information and personal-information leakage risks.

citation.citationTitle: 生成AIなどの利用について
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For students in Waseda University's School of Commerce and Graduate School of Commerce, the July 2023 school-level notice says generative AI tools are not uniformly prohibited, each course instructor decides whether they may be used, and students are not permitted to entrust all submitted work to generative AI.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Commerce guidance allows course-level instructor decisions and forbids wholly AI-generated submitted work.

artifacts.claimText: Waseda's Support Anywhere student FAQ says students must follow the instructor's directions on generative AI use for reports and papers, and should ask the instructor in advance if they are unsure.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Student FAQ routes report and paper generative-AI questions to instructor directions.

artifacts.claimText: Waseda's official Support Anywhere page describes its student-support chatbot as powered by generative AI and warns that answers can be inferred and may differ from the latest information or facts, so important procedures and policies should be checked against official Support Anywhere pages and the student's school or graduate school.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Official Support Anywhere chatbot uses generative AI but is not authoritative for important procedures or policies.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 生成AIなどの利用について
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 生成AIが作成した論文等を学生の皆さんがよく精査せずにそのまま提出した場合、内容に不備や致命的な誤りがあったとしても、それを基に評価がされるばかりでなく、本人が意図せずとも、提出された論文等に剽窃や不適切な引用、捏造等が確認された場合は、当該学生自身が懲戒処分の対象となりうる点にも注意が必要です。もちろん、生成AIが作成した論文等をそのまま提出すれば、それだけでカンニング等と同様の不正行為となり処罰されます。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Waseda warns that submitting unexamined AI-created papers may still be evaluated and can expose the student to discipline if plagiarism, inappropriate citation, or fabrication is found; submitting AI-created papers as-is is misconduct similar to cheating.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: University-wide statement, section on characteristics of generative AI

artifacts.sourceTitle: 生成AIの利用について（商学部・商学研究科）
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: ①生成AIツールの使用を一律で禁止することは行いませんが、慎重に利用することが求められます。②各授業における生成AIツールの利用の可否は各担当教員が決定します。各担当教員の指示に従ってください。③それぞれの授業において設定されている学習目標を達成するためには知識を身につけ、思考力を向上させることが必要です。この観点から、課題等の提出物の作成をすべて生成AIにゆだねることは認められません。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The Commerce notice says generative AI tools are not uniformly prohibited, each instructor decides whether they may be used in each class, students should follow instructor directions, and all submitted work may not be entrusted to generative AI.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Commerce and Graduate School of Commerce notice, numbered guidance items 1-3

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The Support Anywhere chatbot generates answers based on the information published on the Support Anywhere website. However, depending on the content of your question, relevant information may not be found. In such cases, the generative AI (artificial intelligence) may create an answer based on inference. As a result, some answers may differ from the latest information or actual facts. For important procedures or policies, please always refer to the information provided on the official Support Anywhere webpages and your affiliated school or graduate school.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The official student-support page says the chatbot is powered by generative AI and may create inferred answers when relevant information is not found.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Support Anywhere about page, chatbot section

artifacts.notes: Approved because the claim closely follows Waseda's university-wide Japanese statement and does not infer beyond academic-integrity consequences stated in the source.
Approved because the claim is limited to the source's warning about confidential-information and personal-information leakage from careless generative-AI use.
Approved with school-level scope only. The claim explicitly limits the policy to the School of Commerce and Graduate School of Commerce and does not present it as a university-wide rule.
Approved because the Support Anywhere FAQ directly tells students to follow instructor directions and check in advance when unsure about generative AI use.
Approved as a narrow claim about the official Support Anywhere chatbot and its stated limitations; it is not used as evidence for course AI-use policy.

artifacts.title: Waseda University Generative AI Source Review
artifacts.summary: The staged Waseda University crawl found an official university-wide Japanese statement on generative AI, an English translation, Commerce and Graduate School of Commerce school-level guidance, and Support Anywhere student-support guidance. The resulting claims are limited to academic-integrity warnings, confidentiality and personal-information cautions, school-level instructor control for Commerce, student routing to instructor directions, and the official Support Anywhere chatbot's stated limits. Non-policy course/research pages and an inaccessible law-program PDF were rejected or marked inaccessible.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The Graduate School of Law LL.M. AI-guideline PDF was not used as evidence because direct HTTP fetch returned HTTP 403 in this crawl environment.
School-level Commerce guidance is not treated as a university-wide rule.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
